subject: nano-id base58 and base62 functions
truth: Stop using nano_id::base58 and nano_id::base62; they use reduced character sets (16 and 32 symbols instead of 58 and 62), severely cutting entropy. Upgrade to nano-id >= 0.4.0 or use nano_id::base64 which is not affected.
quote: Specifically, the `base62` function used a character set of 32 symbols instead of the intended 62 symbols, and the `base58` function used a character set of 16 symbols instead of the intended 58 symbo

subject: rand_core::le::read_u32_into and read_u64_into buffer length check
truth: Stop using incorrect buffer length checks in rand_core::le::read_u32_into and read_u64_into that allow the destination buffer to be under-filled; use the patched versions >= 0.6.2 which enforce correct bounds.
quote: incorrect checks on the source buffer length, allowing the destination buffer to be under-filled

subject: SqliteConnection::deserialize_readonly_database buffer lifetime
truth: Stop passing a borrowed buffer to SqliteConnection::deserialize_readonly_database without ensuring it outlives the connection; Diesel now stores a copy of the buffer internally to prevent use-after-free.
quote: libsqlite3 requires the buffer to remain alive for as long as the database connection is open and Diesel did not ensure this as part of its safe API

subject: libcrux_aesgcm AES-GCM authentication tag check
truth: Stop using non-constant-time authentication tag comparisons in AES-GCM decryption; use a best-effort constant-time tag check implementation that has been verified to reliably produce constant-time code.
quote: AES-GCM decryption used an implementation for checking the provided authentication tag against the recomputed authentication tag that was intended to be constant-time, but resulted in non-constant-time code generation in certain circumstances.

subject: time crate local offset functions
truth: Stop using time::UtcOffset::local_offset_at and related functions in multithreaded programs on Unix; they set environment variables without synchronization, causing segfaults from dangling pointers.
quote: The affected functions set environment variables without synchronization. On Unix-like operating systems, this can crash in multithreaded programs.

subject: rand::rng() / rand::thread_rng() with custom logger
truth: Stop calling rand::rng() (or rand::thread_rng()) inside a custom logger implementation, because reseeding creates an aliased mutable reference (Undefined Behavior) when the logger re-enters the RNG.
quote: aliased mutable reference, violating the Stacked Borrows rules

subject: Rust cocoon encryption nonce reuse
truth: Stop reusing the same cocoon object for multiple encryption calls; create a new cocoon with a different seed per encryption call, or use Cocoon::new (ThreadRng) instead of MiniCocoon/StdRng.
quote: Sequential calls of encryption API (`encrypt`, `wrap`, and `dump`) result in nonce reuse

subject: UserIdentity::is_verified in matrix-sdk-crypto
truth: Stop using UserIdentity::is_verified() without separately checking the verification status of the user's own identity; the method may return a value contrary to its documentation.
quote: doesn't take into account the verification status of the user's own identity while performing the check and may as a result return a value contrary to what is implied by its name and documentation

subject: destination_is_initially_empty defaults to false, triggering unsafe chmod path
truth: Upgrade gix-worktree-state to >= 0.17.0, which applies the umask correctly on executable-file checkout; do not use destination_is_initially_empty: true as a general fix (safe only when the destination is truly empty).
quote: The default value of `destination_is_initially_empty` is `false`, so some applications may be affected even if they don't attempt checkouts in nonempty directories.
_currency_fix: RUSTSEC-2025-0001 fixed in gix-worktree-state 0.17.0; workaround is not the real fix (verified 2026-07-18)

subject: clean_text output in unquoted HTML attributes
truth: Always quote HTML attributes when interpolating clean_text output, because clean_text preserves Form Feed (U+000C) which HTML treats as whitespace, allowing attribute injection.
quote: Because HTML treats the Form Feed as whitespace, code like this has an injection bug

subject: gix_path::env::installation_config and installation_config_prefix
truth: Stop treating the local repository's git config as installation-scoped when higher scopes are absent; instead, only trust config from system, global, or installation scopes.
quote: mistakenly treats the local repository's configuration as system-wide if no higher scoped configuration is found

subject: libcrux_chacha20poly1305 encrypt buffer overflow panic
truth: Stop passing a ciphertext buffer longer than ptxt.len() + TAG_LEN to encrypt; the fix silently truncates output to the first ptxt.len() + TAG_LEN bytes instead of panicking.
quote: panic in this case, but instead write out the ciphertext and tag into

subject: transmute_vec_as_bytes with non-Pod types
truth: Stop using transmute_vec_as_bytes with generic T that may have padding bytes; require T: Pod (plain old data) instead of T: Copy to prevent uninitialized memory exposure.
quote: The library provides a public safe API `transmute_vec_as_bytes`, which incorrectly assumes that any generic type `T` could have stable layout, causing to uninitialized memory exposure if the users pas

subject: pprof::report::ReportBuilder::build and pprof::validate
truth: Stop using std::slice::from_raw_parts with a generic type without first constraining the type's alignment and validity; perform type checks before unsafe type conversion.
quote: The library breaks the safety assumptions when using unsafe API `std::slice::from_raw_parts`.

subject: theshit loads Python rules from user-writable paths without ownership checks when running as root
truth: Always validate that configuration and rule files are owned by the effective user (root) and not writable by non-root users before loading them with elevated privileges.
quote: continues to trust configuration files originating from the unprivileged user's environment

subject: gix_worktree_state::checkout sets executable files world-writable when destination_is_initially_empty is false
truth: When setting executable permissions on checked-out files, use OpenOptionsEx::mode (which respects umask) instead of PermissionsExt::set_mode with 0777 (which does not).
quote: A mode of 0777 is not safe here, because the umask is not applied.

subject: X25519 all-zero shared secret check in HPKE
truth: Stop computing X25519 shared secrets without checking that the result is non-zero; validate that the shared secret is not all-zero after Diffie-Hellman key exchange.
quote: does not include the check that the key exchange was contributory, i.e. does not ensure on its own that the resulting shared secret is non-zero

subject: libflate MultiDecoder::read drops uninitialized memory on panic
truth: Abort immediately instead of unwinding when a panic occurs in caller-supplied Read implementation within MultiDecoder::read, to prevent drop of uninitialized memory.
quote: This would cause `drop()` to be called on uninitialized memory of a generic type implementing `Read`.

subject: PAX extended header size validation in tokio-tar
truth: Stop using tokio-tar (RUSTSEC-2025-0111, unmaintained, PAX/ustar header desync file-smuggling bug); switch to astral-tokio-tar >=0.6.1 (earlier astral-tokio-tar releases carry their own PAX header desynchronization vulnerabilities — RUSTSEC-2025-0110/CVE-2025-62518 fixed in 0.5.6, and a second issue RUSTSEC-2026-0112 fixed in 0.6.1) or another maintained crate that correctly validates PAX extended header sizes against ustar header size fields.
quote: incorrectly handles PAX extended headers, when the ustar header incorrectly specifies zero size
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The advisory recommends astral-tokio-tar but does not pin a version in this fact; astral-tokio-tar itself had its own PAX header desynchronization vulnerability

subject: memmap2 range validation
truth: Always validate that offset + len does not exceed the mapped file length before calling advise_range, flush_range, or their unchecked variants on Mmap/MmapMut.
quote: Affected versionf of `memmap2` did not perform enough validation on the `offset` and `len` parameters of

subject: DNSSEC DNSKEY RRset validation trusts all keys if any one matches a trust anchor
truth: Stop trusting the entire DNSKEY RRset when only one key matches a trust anchor; validate self-signatures from the trusted key over all other keys in the set.
quote: verify_dnskey_rrset() will return Ok(true) if any record's public key matches a trust anchor

subject: Diesel COPY FROM/TO options
truth: Stop passing user-provided strings to with_delimiter, with_null, with_quote, or with_escape without Diesel 2.3.8+; upgrade to >= 2.3.8 which escapes quotes.
quote: Diesel did not check if any these user-provided options contain a quote character `'`, which can lead to the injection of additional options in the current `COPY FROM`/`COPY TO` statement.

subject: DTriangle accessor bounds check
truth: Stop exposing safe functions that index into fixed-size arrays with get_unchecked without validating bounds; add a bounds check or make the function unsafe.
quote: safe functions that accepted an arbitrary `order` value

subject: DS-authenticated DNSKEY leads to trust in unrelated keys in the same zone
truth: Stop treating DS authentication of one DNSKEY as proof for all DNSKEYs in the zone; pass the authenticated DNSKEYs from the DS check to RRSIG validation.
quote: verify_dnskey_rrset() returns Ok(false) if any DNSKEY record is covered by a DS record

subject: Compiler optimizations eliminating unsound writes through immutable reference in glib
truth: Do not rely on C functions mutating a pointer through an immutable reference (&p); the compiler may optimize away the writes, causing NULL dereferences.
quote: After changes in recent versions of the Rust compiler, these unsound writes through `&p` now seem to be completely disregarded when building the `glib` crate with optimizations.

subject: std::process::Command on Windows resolves relative binary names by checking CWD first
truth: Stop passing relative binary names to std::process::Command on Windows; construct Command with an absolute binary name or use grep_cli::resolve_binary.
quote: a quirk of the Windows process execution API is that it will automatically consider the current directory before other directories when resolving relative binary names

subject: Passing immutable reference to out-argument pointer in glib::VariantStrIter
truth: Pass a mutable reference (&mut p) instead of an immutable reference (&p) when passing a pointer as an out-argument to a C function that mutates it.
quote: This was fixed by passing the out-argument pointer explitly as `&mut p` instead of `&p`.

subject: FieldVar::mul_by_inverse in ark-r1cs-std
truth: Stop using FieldVar::mul_by_inverse without verifying it enforces constraints; upgrade to ark-r1cs-std >= 0.3.1 which adds the missing constraint.
quote: Versions `0.2.0` to `0.3.0` of ark-r1cs-std did not enforce any constraints in the `FieldVar::mul_by_inverse` method, allowing a malicious prover to produce an unsound proof that passes all verifier c

subject: lettre SMTP body period-escaping
truth: Stop using naive period-escaping that fails after double CRLF; use the library's fixed escaping that correctly handles consecutive CRLF sequences.
quote: The module for escaping lines starting with a period wouldn't catch a period that was placed after a double CRLF sequence

subject: libcrux_aesgcm AES-GCM AAD length limit
truth: Enforce NIST SP 800-38D's AAD bit-length limit of 2^64 - 1 bits in AES-GCM encryption and decryption, rejecting overlong AAD inputs with an error.
quote: neither enforced this limit for encryption nor for decryption

subject: once_cell::sync::Lazy initialization panic leads to undefined behavior
truth: Stop using Lazy::force or Lazy::deref if the initialization function can panic; use std::sync::OnceLock or ensure the init function never panics.
quote: If during the first dereference of Lazy<T> the initialization function panics,

subject: age plugin name parsing does not validate path separators
truth: Stop using plugin names from untrusted input without validating they contain no path separators; use a allowlist of known plugin names instead.
quote: A plugin name containing a path separator may allow an attacker to execute an arbitrary binary.

subject: grep_cli::DecompressionReader::new resolves binary names via Windows API, checking CWD before PATH
truth: Stop relying on Windows API to resolve binary names; use grep_cli::resolve_binary to resolve decompression programs to absolute paths via PATH.
quote: using `PATH`, instead of relying on the Windows API to do it.

subject: i_tree Tree::node and Tree::mut_node
truth: Always validate index bounds before calling Vec::get_unchecked; do not expose safe public methods that pass unchecked indices to get_unchecked.
quote: These methods accepted an arbitrary `u32` index and passed it directly to `Vec::get_unchecked` / `get_unchecked_mut` on the internal node buffer, without validating that the index was in bounds.

subject: Diesel SQLite string deserialization with from_utf8_unchecked
truth: Stop using str::from_utf8_unchecked on sqlite3_value_text output; always validate UTF-8 with str::from_utf8 before constructing a Rust &str.
quote: Based on that we used `str::from_utf8_unchecked` to construct a Rust string slice without any additional UTF-8 checks in place.

subject: aes-gcm decrypt_in_place_detached exposes plaintext on tag failure
truth: After decrypt_in_place_detached returns an error, zeroize or re-encrypt the buffer to prevent plaintext exposure on authentication failure.
quote: the decrypted ciphertext (i.e. the correct plaintext) is exposed even if tag verification fails

subject: gix-path hard-coded fallback paths on Windows
truth: Stop using hard-coded fallback paths for finding git.exe on Windows; instead only search PATH or use a safe, validated discovery mechanism.
quote: When looking for Git for Windows so it can run it to report its paths, `gix-path` can be tricked into running another `git.exe` placed in an untrusted location by a limited user account.

subject: OpenSSL X509 extension builders parse input as mini-language allowing arbitrary file reads
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings to SubjectAlternativeName::new or ExtendedKeyUsage::other; use the type-safe builder methods instead.
quote: parses all input using an OpenSSL mini-language which can perform arbitrary file reads

subject: lettre sendmail transport argument injection
truth: Stop passing user-controlled email addresses directly as arguments to the sendmail command; use '--' to separate options from addresses.
quote: It was possible, using forged `to` addresses,

subject: Vec::set_len on uninitialized vector in HDR decoder
truth: Stop calling Vec::set_len on uninitialized vectors with user-provided type parameters; ensure proper initialization before set_len.
quote: Affected versions of this crate would call `Vec::set_len` on an uninitialized

subject: intaglio SymbolTable intern after hasher panic
truth: Make vec mutation transactional across unwind boundaries so partially inserted entries are rolled back before the panic propagates.
quote: The `intern` implementations committed a `vec.push(...)` before the matching

subject: Pre-allocating output buffer as mutable slice instead of unsafe Vec manipulation
truth: Stop using unsafe Vec::set_len for output buffers; pre-allocate the buffer and pass a mutable slice to avoid unsafe code entirely.
quote: pre-allocate the output buffer and pass a mutable slice instead, avoiding all unsafe code

subject: libgit2-sys (git_revparse_single infinite loop DoS)
truth: Stop using git_revparse_single on untrusted input without a timeout; upgrade to libgit2-sys >= 0.16.2 (bundles libgit2 >= 1.7.2).
quote: * The `git_revparse_single` function can potentially enter an infinite loop on a well-crafted input, potentially causing a Denial of Service.

subject: libsecp256k1 Scalar::check_overflow timing side-channel
truth: Stop using non-constant-time Scalar::check_overflow; upgrade to libsecp256k1 >= 0.3.1 which executes the check in constant time.
quote: did not execute Scalar::check_overflow in constant time

subject: SHA-1 hashing in gitoxide
truth: Stop using plain SHA-1 without collision detection; use sha1collisiondetection (sha1_checked) to detect known SHA-1 collisions.
quote: gitoxide uses the `sha1_smol` or `sha1` crate, both of which implement standard SHA-1 without any mitigations for collision attacks.

subject: hpke-rs Context::export length truncation
truth: Stop passing length > 65535 to Context::export; the length value is silently cast to u16, truncating any value exceeding 65535.
quote: length value is cast to `u16` truncating any value exceeding

subject: AVX2 SHAKE-256 output buffer length not multiple of 8
truth: Stop assuming output buffer lengths are always multiples of 8; validate or pad buffer lengths before passing to AVX2 SHAKE-256.
quote: The AVX2-optimized implementation of SHAKE-256

subject: Hive::close and Drop both free
truth: Stop calling Hive::close before drop; the Drop implementation already frees the handle, so calling close causes a double-free.
quote: `Drop` implementation for `Hive` did perform free, but so did `Hive::close`, which,

subject: Diesel SQLite BLOB misinterpreted as UTF-8 string
truth: Stop assuming sqlite3_value_text returns valid UTF-8 for BLOB fields; validate the byte buffer before constructing a string.
quote: this function doesn't always return correct UTF-8 strings

subject: shlex::quote/join with control characters for interactive shells
truth: Do not pass shlex::quote output to an interactive shell's stdin; control characters can cause arbitrary command injection.
quote: The `quote` family of functions does not and cannot escape control characters.

subject: Triton VM sponge_absorb_mem instruction
truth: Always include constraints that verify hashed values come from the claimed memory location when using sponge_absorb_mem.
quote: fails to verify that hashed values come from the claimed memory location

subject: SmallVec::grow with value less than current capacity
truth: Never call SmallVec::grow with a value less than the current capacity; this corrupts memory allocator data structures.
quote: Attempting to call `grow` on a spilled SmallVec with a value less than the current capacity causes corruption of memory allocator data structures.

subject: SQL injection in SqliteEventCacheStore::find_event_with_relations
truth: Stop using format!() to build SQL queries with user input; use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead.
quote: queries using `format!()` with unescaped input, allowing an attacker to inject

subject: Webhook::verify signature comparison
truth: Stop comparing signatures of different lengths; use a constant-time comparison that also checks lengths are equal.
quote: incorrectly compared signatures of different lengths - the two signatures would only be compared up to the length of the shorter signature

subject: Triton VM FRI verifier randomness sampling
truth: Stop using triton_vm::verify from versions < 2.0.0; upgrade to >= 2.0.0 where FRI randomness is sampled correctly.
quote: the verifier failed to correctly sample randomness in the FRI sub-protocol

subject: futures_task::waker missing 'static bound
truth: Always ensure the data passed to futures_task::waker satisfies 'static lifetime bound to prevent use-after-free.
quote: did not properly implement a `'static` lifetime bound on the `waker` function

subject: libgit2-sys (git_index_add heap corruption)
truth: Stop using git_index_add on untrusted index data; upgrade to libgit2-sys >= 0.16.2 (bundles libgit2 >= 1.7.2).
quote: * The `git_index_add` function may cause heap corruption and possibly lead to arbitrary code execution.

subject: PeerRecord::from_signed_envelope public key verification
truth: Stop accepting PeerRecord without verifying that the public key in the SignedEnvelope matches the PeerId.
quote: did not check that the public key the signature was created with matches the peer ID of the peer record

subject: better-macro crate
truth: Do not use the better-macro crate; it is a fake crate with deliberate RCE capability via proc-macros.
quote: "Proving A Point" that proc-macros can run arbitrary code.

subject: gix_path::env git config path parsing
truth: Stop stripping quotation marks from git-reported paths; use --null/-z to get unquoted literal paths.
quote: they attempt to parse the path by stripping the quotation marks

subject: TOTP token comparison in totp-rs
truth: Stop comparing TOTP tokens with non-constant-time comparison; use constant_time_eq for verification.
quote: did not compare tokens in constant time

subject: fuser::Session::new passes NULL operations list to libfuse
truth: Stop passing NULL as the operations list to fuse_session_new; always pass a valid operations list.
quote: operation list was passed as NULL incorrectly

subject: gix_path::env::installation_config and installation_config_prefix
truth: Stop parsing quoted paths from git output manually; pass -z/--null to git to get literal paths.
quote: Affected versions of `gix-path` do not pass `-z`/`--null` to cause `git` to report literal paths ([`650a1b5`](https://github.com/Byron/gitoxide/commit/650a1b5cf25e086197cc55a68525a411e1c28031)).

subject: Parc/Prc projection from Arc/Rc without 'static bound
truth: Require T: 'static when creating Parc/Prc projections from Arc/Rc to prevent use-after-free.
quote: Affected versions of this crate didn't provide sufficient lifetime constraints to conversion

subject: lzf::compress and lzf::decompress use mem::uninitialized
truth: Stop using mem::uninitialized to create arrays; use a heap-allocated Vec instead.
quote: to create an array of uninitialized values, to later write values into it.

subject: hpke-rs Context::seal/open with HpkeExport AEAD
truth: Return an error instead of allowing seal/open on an export-only HPKE context.
quote: Constructing an HPKE Context with the AEAD algorithm set to `HpkeExport` resulted in a panic when calling `Context::seal`, or `Context::open`
